summary: Post-refactor regression of the FOUNDATION SCP Site Overseer CLI game. Built /app/backend/tests/test_foundation_scp.py (pytest, 29 tests across CLI smoke, data integrity, secrets-backed RNG, GameState actions, loadout capacity, breach assess/_resolve formulas, Archives CRUD/persistence, cross-process persistence). Also drove the CLI via stdin for hub→Operations and hub→Breach (SCP-999) flows. All 29 tests PASS, no tracebacks, save_data.json + archives.json created and re-loaded across separate `python -m game.main` invocations. The breach.py / hub.py / rng.py refactor preserves all observable behavior.
critical_code_review_comments: game/state.py operation_success_chance() shadows the inner var `chance` (same name as game.rng.chance imported at module top). Harmless because rng.chance isn't used in this method, but renaming to `pct` would prevent future confusion.
game/state.py try_run_operation() also shadows `chance` with the local int — same readability concern.
game/breach.py _assess() also locally reuses `chance` as a return name; fine but uniformly renaming to `survival_pct` across breach/state would clarify intent.

game/archives.py update() silently no-ops when entry_id is not found (returns None). UI layer (_archive_edit) only reaches it via _pick_index so it's safe today, but adding a defensive `raise KeyError` or returning a boolean for callers that don't pre-validate would be more robust.

game/rng.py roll(low, high) does not validate low <= high; secrets.randbelow(0 or negative) would raise ValueError. Add `if high < low: raise ValueError` for clarity if this is ever called with computed bounds.

game/main.py top-level try/except catches KeyboardInterrupt/EOFError but does NOT save state on those exit paths — Ctrl+C mid-session loses progress since saves are only triggered on the 'q' branch (and after each action). Most actions save individually so risk is small, but consider a final state.save()/archives.save() in the except block.

game/hub.py archives_menu() uses _ARCHIVE_ACTIONS dispatch but archives_menu does not pass `state`; if any archive action ever needs state (e.g. rank-gated archive features), the signature will need to change. Minor future-proofing note only.
test_credentials: None — no auth in this app.
seed_data_creation: Each test starts by deleting /app/saves/save_data.json and /app/saves/archives.json (autouse fixture). Test-created Archive entries use TEST_ prefix and are wiped on teardown.
context_for_next_testing_agent:
This project is a pure-Python terminal game under /app/game (NOT a web app, NO FastAPI, NO MongoDB, NO frontend). Entry point: `cd /app && python -m game.main` or `python play.py`. Saves live under /app/saves/{save_data.json, archives.json}. Tests live at /app/backend/tests/test_foundation_scp.py and run via `pytest backend/tests/ -v`. To drive the CLI in subprocess, pipe stdin like: `printf '
q
' | python -m game.main` (the first '
' clears the initial title-banner pause). Menus are number-keyed, prompts are [y/n]. Breach menu indexes are 1-based, mapping to SCPS where breachable==True; e.g. index 9 == SCP-999 (threat 1, easy survival). Operations is data.OPERATIONS in JSON order; op_SCP_999 has required_rank 0. Use `pip install -r requirements.txt`-less setup: the only runtime dependency is `rich` and it's already importable. The previous iteration_1.json file in /app/test_reports is from an unrelated template scaffold and can be ignored.
rca of the issue: No bugs found. Refactor preserved behavior — secrets-backed RNG still produces values in expected ranges; _assess() math matches the documented formula clamp(10..95, 60 - threat*8 + rank*5 + loadout_bonus); _ARCHIVE_ACTIONS dispatch dict works; status-cell helpers render proper DONE/AVAILABLE/RANK LOCK/NEED RC tokens; persistence verified across separate process runs.
